lines.text: COPY
10 November 1950
On 9 November, 1950, [REDACTED]), N.E.P.A.
Division, Fairchild Engine and Airplane Corporation, Oak Ridge, Tennessee, stated
substantially as follows: Between 1155 and 1200 on 5 November 1950, [REDACTED]
observed an object flying over Oak Ridge, Tennessee. During the past six months
[REDACTED] has made illustrations from a vantage point at the intersection of [REDACTED]
[REDACTED], at Oak Ridge, Tennessee, and he was therefore quite
familiar with the terrain and various features of the locality. At the time of
[REDACTED] observation he was adjacent to house number 322 West Outer Drive and he
described the object as a "wiggly, fat cell, with characteristics of a toy balloon.
However, this object has a substantial outline and its mass remained constant
although the shape of the object changed rapidly. The object made quick movements
in the general vicinity above the ridge shown on the attached illustration, which
ridge is approximately six miles away. [REDACTED] was unable to judge the distance
of the object from his vantage point, however, it appeared to be over the ridge
but could have been either closer or farther. If the object was over the ridge
(Six miles away,) it would have been approximately the size of a thirteen story
building. [REDACTED] noticed no light or glow emitting from the object and it appeared
to have no inner lighting. Attached hereto is an illustration on which there are
certified true notations made by [REDACTED] at the time and place of his
observation.
The following notes are quoted:
"No F-82 (fighter type aircraft). Must be some phenomenon of light
on clouds." [REDACTED] explained that the object itself did not appear
to be a phenomenon of light on clouds but rather was affected by light
on clouds.)
"Rapidly changing shape - constant mass - translucent appearance -
sharply outlined edges - gray color, cool in contrast with warm,
pinkish gray of clouds - no spectral reflection of light, no bright
highlights - definite suggestion of form - movements rapidly, darting
in all directions within small area - seemed to be moving in and out
of clouds (this may have been because of semi-transparent characteristics).
Movements did not seem to be result of wind. First impression - balloon
but too rapid and random in movement - too rapid and extreme in change
of shape. Seemed to be this side of mountain (Waldron Ridge). Halluci-
tion observed for five to ten minutes. Very clear. No haze. Compass
bearing from my position 305 degrees. Date - 11-5-50. Time - 1155 -
67C [REDACTED] stated that the core (triangular shape dark portion of the illustration)
remained constant and the apex of the core varied only a few degrees while the body
of the object seemed to change shapes rapidly and would become elongated during a
quick movement of the object.
